texto: A banal mysticism, which is so banal that all the mysticism seems to have evaporated long ago, binds 'us' to the homeland - that special place which is more than a place, more than a geophysical area.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Billig sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “A banal mysticism, which is so banal that all the mysticism seems to have evaporated long ago, bi…” articula reflexao, belief, mysticism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mysticism e nationalism.

frase_original: A banal mysticism, which is so banal that all the mysticism seems to have evaporated long ago, binds 'us' to the homeland - that special place which is more than a place, more than a geophysical area.

texto: The sports pages are men's pages, although they are not presented as such. /.../ On foreign fields, the men win their trophies, or lose their honour, doing battle on the nation's behalf. The readers, mainly men, are invited to see these male exploits in terms of the whole homeland, and, thus, men's concerns are presented as if defining the whole national honour.The parallel between sport and warfare seems obvious...
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Billig: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The sports pages are men's pages, although they are not presented as such. /.../ On foreign field…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, nationalism, sport pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de sport e sports.
